criteria_checked.criterion_ref: sub-gate 3 — every ledger-minted receipt passes v0.6.2 validate (plan L236-238)
criteria_checked.note: dmc-evidence-ledger.py build_receipt emits kind=evidence_receipt, PRODUCER_MILESTONE_ID="v0.6.2", identical EVIDENCE_TYPES set, and BIND fields (work_id/plan_hash/repo_hash/verification_ref); dmc-v0.6.2-evidence-receipt.py validate_receipt's required fields are all satisfied. Shape-compatible; the row is implementable.
dmc-stop-gate.py EXIT_PASS=0 / EXIT_HOLD=4 confirmed; the alias-tier subprocess mapping is faithful and the M6 hook stays on stop-gate quick (frozen surface untouched).

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: sub-gate input shapes: findings {subject,findings}, goal {ledger,completion}, decision answer Q1-Q6 (plan L239-245)
criteria_checked.note: dmc-v0.6.3 gate(claim) reads subject+findings; dmc-v0.6.4 trace(obj) reads ledger+completion; dmc-v0.6.5-decision-trace.py answer(record) emits Q1-Q6 from the record. All match.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: scope-lock adjudicate 0/3 + diff-scope base honesty (plan L75-82,L220-228)
criteria_checked.note: dmc-scope-lock.py --adjudicate allow=0/refuse=3; run-lifecycle repo_hash = git status --porcelain|sha256 (NOT a commit sha) — the committed-diff blindness + --base escape hatch are honestly disclosed; postbash layer-B sealed-trust semantics match the structural-REFUSE tier.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: gate-checks v0.2.6 --gate commit not a mktemp flake (plan L229-232)
criteria_checked.note: G6 upstream is INFO-only under gate=commit; it only sets fail=1 on the push gate. No-upstream mktemp repo does not spuriously fail G6.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: top-level gate verb free; linkcheck resolves it (plan L69-70,L303-312)
criteria_checked.note: No top-level gate) arm exists (existing gate) is nested under verdict); linkcheck dispatcher_verbs excludes nested arms by depth, so a new top-level gate) registers cleanly. VERB_RE first-token confirms dmc gate release -> verb gate.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: delegation.schema.md editable without breaking mirror/selftest (plan L94-101,L293-301)
criteria_checked.note: No root DELEGATION_SCHEMA.md mirror pair exists; git log --follow shows exactly 1 commit; dmc-delegation.py self-test references the schema only in a docstring (not read at runtime). Surgical prose additions are safe; selftest delegation 41/0 preserved.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: CI steps 4/6/8 empirically pass today: doctor exit 0, model-name grep empty, fetch-depth 0 (plan L316-343)
criteria_checked.note: bin/dmc doctor exits 0 (Result: PASS) on this repo; the scoped model-name grep with --exclude=models.json --exclude=dmc-roles.py returns empty; git merge-base --is-ancestor 2999870 HEAD = 0, confirming fetch-depth:0 is required and sufficient for the m6 restore/rollback fixtures.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: chain sub-gate honesty — provenance-tier vs mutation guarantee (plan L83-88,L251-261)
criteria_checked.note: scope-guard RUN_STATE_BASENAMES and postbash INTEGRITY_BASENAMES do NOT cover delegations.jsonl/authorizations (run-dir append-log exemption), so an actor deleting both hides worker-apply activity and the predicate returns PASS-with-note. This hole is DISCLOSED by the plan (sub-gate 8 + risk row) and the actual mutation floor is diff-scope + Ring-1 postbash — honest-scope compliant, not over-claimed.

Release checklist is NOT required (extension point only); the five composed tools, M6 hooks/settings, and lifecycle core are all no-edit. Rollback is additive. Plan validator: VALID against dmc.plan-instance.v1.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: CI step 7 forbidden-lexeme scope (plan L335-339) vs cited M8 :507
criteria_checked.note: Plan prose says 'no dangerously-bypass-hook-trust anywhere', but its cited authority (M8 plan :507) scopes that lexeme to .claude/install + bin/lib/dmc-doctor.py, and the lexeme legitimately occurs in ~20 tracked files. A literal repo-wide grep guarantees a permanently red CI — defeating the plan's flagship 'CI green on branch' / Option-A boundary criterion. See B1.

criteria_checked.criterion_ref: green-path gate-checks all-9-PASS requires staged allowlist (plan L362-370,L384-398)
criteria_checked.note: v0.2.6 G2 (allowlist fully staged) FAILs on an unstaged worktree; neither green-path proof specifies a git-add of scope.lock files[].path before gate release --full, so gate-checks would FAIL -> overall FAIL. Implementable but under-specified. See A1.

blockers.statement: CI step 7 as written contradicts its own cited authority and repo reality: the plan says 'no `dangerously-bypass-hook-trust` anywhere' (step 7), but the referenced M8 :507 grep scopes that lexeme to `.claude/install bin/lib/dmc-doctor.py` only, and the lexeme legitimately appears in ~20 tracked files (adapters/codex/README.md:59, .codex/config.toml:14, docs/CODEX_ADAPTER.md:104/186/217, plus evidence/plan files). If the CI step is implemented as a literal repo-wide grep for that lexeme, dmc-ci.yml is guaranteed red on every run, making the required 'CI green on branch' acceptance criterion — the plan's self-described doubly-load-bearing Option-A enforcement boundary — unachievable. Pin the exact scope: grep the bypass lexeme (and the network/credential set) ONLY over `.claude/install` + `bin/lib/dmc-doctor.py` per M8 :507, NOT 'anywhere'; and reconcile whether the M8 :507 set or the M9 prose subset (which drops nc/netcat, smtplib, ftplib, api., CODEX_API_KEY, GLM_API_KEY) is the authoritative lexeme list.

advisories.statement: gate-checks (sub-gate 2) invokes v0.2.6 with `--gate commit`; G2 (allowlist fully staged) FAILs on an unstaged working tree. Neither green-path proof (test-release-gate.sh green rows; test-e2e-loop.sh) specifies a staging step (git add of exactly scope.lock files[].path) before `gate release --full`, so on an unstaged tree gate-checks FAILs -> overall FAIL, contradicting the all-9-sub-gates-PASS expectation. Pin the staging precondition in both fixtures (or have the composer stage a temp index) so the green path can pass gate-checks.

The chain sub-gate (8) predicate is provenance-tier only: scope-guard RUN_STATE_BASENAMES (scope.lock.json/approvals.jsonl/run.json/blocked.json/snapshot.txt) and postbash INTEGRITY_BASENAMES (scope.lock.json/run.json/approvals.jsonl/snapshot.txt) do NOT cover delegations.jsonl or authorizations/*.json, and the run-dir append-log exemption permits Bash writes/deletes there, so an actor deleting both a delegation line and its authorization yields PASS-with-note. The plan discloses this honestly, but release-readiness.schema.md must explicitly label the chain sub-gate as accountability/provenance (diff-scope + Ring-1 postbash are the actual mutation-detection floor), not a guarantee that all applied changes were chained.

Prose label mismatch: the plan calls the v0.6.5 tool 'decision-traceability' (L59) but the actual file is bin/lib/dmc-v0.6.5-decision-trace.py. The Relevant Files table uses the version glob `dmc-v0.6.{2,3,4,5}-*.{sh,py}` so no wrong path is hardcoded, but pin the real filename in the composer's subprocess invocation to avoid a not-found at runtime.

The E2E full-loop (test-e2e-loop.sh) requires the run's repo_hash/plan_hash to be hash-shaped (>=16 hex) for v0.6.2 receipt validation to pass; since run-lifecycle repo_hash = sha256(git status --porcelain) this holds, but the green-path fixture must ensure every minted receipt carries evidence_type in the five-type set and a resolvable verification_ref, or sub-gate 3 (receipts) FAILs. Confirm the fixture's mint calls set evidence_type to a member of {verification-report,test-result,artifact-existence,review-packet,audit-report}.
